owasp_top10: API3:2023 - Broken Object Property Level Authorization
cwe: CWE-1287 - Improper Validation of Specified Type of Input

threat.title: OpenAPI Schema Manipulation and Spoofing
threat.description: Attackers exploit weaknesses in OpenAPI schema validation by manipulating request payloads to bypass authentication, inject additional properties via mass assignment, leverage type coercion vulnerabilities, or exploit missing schema enforcement. When APIs fail to properly validate against their OpenAPI specifications with strict schemas, attackers can inject unauthorized fields, bypass required field validation, exploit additionalProperties:true defaults, and manipulate data types to gain unauthorized access or elevate privileges.

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker analyzes publicly exposed OpenAPI/Swagger documentation endpoint (/swagger.json, /openapi.json, /api-docs)
2. Identifies schema definition with additionalProperties:true (default) or missing required field validations
3. Crafts malicious payload adding 'isAdmin': true or 'role': 'administrator' to user registration/update request
4. Exploits type coercion by sending string '1' where boolean true expected, bypassing strict validation
5. Server-side mass assignment automatically binds unauthorized properties to internal user object
6. Gains administrative privileges or bypasses authentication checks due to schema pollution
prerequisites: OpenAPI specification exposed or discoverable (Swagger UI, /api-docs endpoint)
Schema validation either missing or uses additionalProperties:true (OAS 3.x default)
API implementation uses auto-binding/mass assignment (e.g., Object.assign, model.from_dict)
No explicit property allowlisting or schema enforcement with additionalProperties:false
Type coercion enabled in validation library without strict mode

cve_references.description: Remote Code Execution via Swagger Parameter Injection - Swagger allowed parameter injection leading to RCE

real_world_breaches.impact: Critical authorization bypass allowing unauthenticated AD user enumeration when AD integration enabled
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Insufficient OpenAPI schema validation allowed unauthorized endpoint access without authentication

real_world_breaches.impact: 99% of organizations reported API security incidents in 2024, with authorization bypass accounting for 95% of API attacks
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Weak schema validation and missing property-level authorization checks in OpenAPI implementations

real_world_breaches.impact: 40% increase in API vulnerabilities year-over-year, with schema validation bypasses as primary attack vector
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Default additionalProperties:true in OpenAPI 3.x and inadequate validation implementations

bug_bounty_reports.summary: GraphQL schema manipulation combined with OpenAPI validation bypass allowing authentication bypass via missing required fields and type coercion
Mass assignment via additionalProperties exploitation - attacker added 'isAdmin' field to user profile update bypassing OpenAPI schema validation

security_research.title: OWASP API Security Top 10 2023 - API3 Broken Object Property Level Authorization
security_research.key_finding: Mass assignment and excessive data exposure (now combined as API3:2023) result from lack of schema-based validation with proper property filtering

security_research.title: API Schema Pollution: How Malformed Requests Bypass Validation
security_research.key_finding: Schema pollution exploits fundamental assumptions applications make about incoming data structures, bypassing validation through type confusion and property injection

security_research.title: Swagger API Vulnerabilities and Exploitation
security_research.key_finding: Misconfigured Swagger instances lead to DOM XSS, HTML injection, and open redirects. Publicly deployed Swagger UI without authentication exposes sensitive endpoints

mitigations.title: Enforce Strict Schema Validation with additionalProperties:false
mitigations.description: Configure OpenAPI schemas to explicitly reject any properties not defined in the specification by setting additionalProperties:false for all request body schemas. This prevents mass assignment attacks and property injection.

code_example.code:
# Secure OpenAPI Schema Definition
openapi: 3.1.0
info:
  title: Secure User API
  version: 1.0.0

paths:
  /users:
    post:
      summary: Create new user
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - username
                - email
                - password
              properties:
                username:
                  type: string
                  minLength: 3
                  maxLength: 50
                  pattern: '^[a-zA-Z0-9_]+$'
                email:
                  type: string
                  format: email
                  maxLength: 255
                password:
                  type: string
                  format: password
                  minLength: 12
              # CRITICAL: Prevent property injection
              additionalProperties: false
      responses:
        '201':
          description: User created
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  id:
                    type: string
                    format: uuid
                  username:
                    type: string
                  email:
                    type: string
                  createdAt:
                    type: string
                    format: date-time
                # Only return safe fields
                additionalProperties: false

iso27001_controls: A.14.1.3 - Protection of application services transactions
nist_csf: PR.DS-5 - Protections against data leaks are implemented
PR.IP-2 - A System Development Life Cycle is implemented
validation_steps: Review all OpenAPI schema definitions for missing additionalProperties:false
Use Spectral linter with custom rule: 'schemas-additionalProperties-false'
Test by sending request with extra fields - should return 400 Bad Request
Verify error messages don't expose internal schema structure

mitigations.title: Implement Request Validation with express-openapi-validator (Node.js)
mitigations.description: Use express-openapi-validator middleware to automatically validate all incoming requests against OpenAPI specification before they reach route handlers. This provides defense-in-depth against schema manipulation attacks.
nist_csf: DE.CM-1 - Network monitored to detect anomalies
validation_steps: Install: npm install express-openapi-validator
Configure with strict validation options (coerceTypes:false, removeAdditional:false)
Test with malicious payloads: extra fields, wrong types, missing required
Verify 400 errors returned with appropriate messages
Test security handlers enforce authentication/authorization

mitigations.title: Implement Schema Validation with Spring Boot and springdoc-openapi
mitigations.description: Configure Spring Boot applications to validate requests against OpenAPI specifications using springdoc-openapi and Jakarta Bean Validation with strict settings.
nist_csf: PR.IP-2 - System Development Life Cycle implemented
validation_steps: Add springdoc-openapi-starter-webmvc-ui dependency
Configure Jackson with failOnUnknownProperties(true)
Add @Valid to all request body parameters
Test with extra properties - should return 400
Verify Swagger UI at /swagger-ui.html shows accurate schema

mitigations.title: Implement Strict Validation with FastAPI and Pydantic
mitigations.description: Leverage FastAPI's built-in Pydantic validation with strict mode to prevent type coercion and property injection attacks.

code_example.code:
from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException, Depends, status
from fastapi.security import HTTPBearer, HTTPAuthorizationCredentials
from pydantic import BaseModel, EmailStr, Field, ConfigDict, field_validator
from typing import Annotated
from datetime import datetime
import re

app = FastAPI(
    title="Secure User API",
    version="1.0.0",
    openapi_version="3.1.0"
)

security = HTTPBearer()

# Strict Pydantic model - prevents property injection and type coercion
class CreateUserRequest(BaseModel):
    """User creation request with strict validation"""
    
    # CRITICAL: Strict mode prevents type coercion attacks
    model_config = ConfigDict(
        extra='forbid',  # Reject any extra fields (additionalProperties:false)
        str_strip_whitespace=True,
        strict=True  # Disable type coercion (prevent "1" -> 1, "true" -> True)
    )
    
    username: Annotated[
        str,
        Field(
            min_length=3,
            max_length=50,
            pattern=r'^[a-zA-Z0-9_]+$',
            description="Unique username (alphanumeric and underscores only)",
            examples=["john_doe"]
        )
    ]
    
    email: Annotated[
        EmailStr,
        Field(
            max_length=255,
            description="Valid email address",
            examples=["john@example.com"]
        )
    ]
    
    password: Annotated[
        str,
        Field(
            min_length=12,
            description="Strong password (min 12 chars)",
            json_schema_extra={'format': 'password'}
        )
    ]
    
    @field_validator('password')
    @classmethod
    def validate_password_strength(cls, v: str) -> str:
        """Ensure password meets security requirements"""
        if not re.search(r'[A-Z]', v):
            raise ValueError('Password must contain uppercase letter')
        if not re.search(r'[a-z]', v):
            raise ValueError('Password must contain lowercase letter')
        if not re.search(r'[0-9]', v):
            raise ValueError('Password must contain digit')
        if not re.search(r'[!@#$%^&*(),.?":{}|<>]', v):
            raise ValueError('Password must contain special character')
        return v

class UserResponse(BaseModel):
    """Safe user response - only public fields"""
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra='forbid')
    
    id: str = Field(description="User UUID")
    username: str
    email: EmailStr
    created_at: datetime
    # NEVER expose: password, role, is_admin, internal_id, etc.

@app.post(
    "/users",
    response_model=UserResponse,
    status_code=status.HTTP_201_CREATED,
    responses={
        201: {"description": "User created successfully"},
        400: {"description": "Validation failed"},
        422: {"description": "Unprocessable entity - schema violation"}
    }
)
async def create_user(user_data: CreateUserRequest) -> UserResponse:
    """
    Create new user with strict schema validation.
    
    Extra properties are rejected, types are strictly validated,
    and all constraints are enforced.
    """
    
    # At this point, user_data is guaranteed to:
    # 1. Contain ONLY username, email, password
    # 2. Have correct types (no type coercion)
    # 3. Pass all validation constraints
    
    # Create user with explicit property assignment (defense in depth)
    user = await create_user_in_db(
        username=user_data.username,
        email=user_data.email,
        password_hash=hash_password(user_data.password),
        role="user",  # NEVER from request
        is_admin=False,  # NEVER from request
        created_at=datetime.utcnow()
    )
    
    return UserResponse(
        id=str(user.id),
        username=user.username,
        email=user.email,
        created_at=user.created_at
    )

@app.get("/users/{user_id}", response_model=UserResponse)
async def get_user(
    user_id: str,
    credentials: HTTPAuthorizationCredentials = Depends(security)
) -> UserResponse:
    """Get user by ID (authenticated)"""
    
    # Validate JWT token
    current_user = verify_jwt(credentials.credentials)
    
    # Authorization check
    if current_user.id != user_id and not current_user.is_admin:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN,
            detail="Insufficient permissions"
        )
    
    user = await get_user_from_db(user_id)
    if not user:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND,
            detail="User not found"
        )
    
    return UserResponse(
        id=str(user.id),
        username=user.username,
        email=user.email,
        created_at=user.created_at
    )

# Custom exception handler for validation errors
from fastapi.exceptions import RequestValidationError
from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse

@app.exception_handler(RequestValidationError)
async def validation_exception_handler(request, exc):
    """Custom error response for validation failures"""
    return JSONResponse(
        status_code=status.HTTP_422_UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY,
        content={
            "error": "Validation failed",
            "message": "Request does not match API specification",
            # In production, don't expose detailed errors
            "details": exc.errors() if app.debug else None
        }
    )

validation_steps: Install: pip install fastapi pydantic[email] uvicorn
Configure Pydantic models with extra='forbid' and strict=True
Test with extra properties - should return 422
Test type coercion bypass attempts (string '1' for boolean)
Verify OpenAPI schema at /docs shows additionalProperties:false

mitigations.title: Disable Public Access to OpenAPI Documentation in Production
mitigations.description: Remove or secure Swagger UI and OpenAPI specification endpoints in production environments to prevent attackers from analyzing API structure and identifying attack vectors.
iso27001_controls: A.14.1.3 - Protection of application services transactions
validation_steps: Verify /api-docs, /swagger-ui.html endpoints return 404 in production
Confirm /openapi.json is not publicly accessible
If docs needed, implement IP allowlisting or OAuth2 authentication
Use Shodan or similar to verify endpoints not exposed: 'swagger site:yourdomain.com'

indicators: HTTP 422 or 400 errors with validation failure messages indicating extra properties
Multiple requests with varying property combinations testing schema boundaries
Requests containing administrative fields (isAdmin, role, permissions, privileges)
Type mismatch attempts in requests (strings for booleans, integers for strings)
Unusual property names matching internal database fields (_id, internal_role, created_by_system)
Successful privilege escalation following schema validation errors

log_queries.query:
// Detect mass assignment and schema manipulation attempts
AppServiceHTTPLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| where ResultCode in (400, 422) // Validation failures
| extend RequestBody = parse_json(RequestBody)
| extend SuspiciousFields = bag_keys(RequestBody) has_any ("isAdmin", "role", "admin", "privileges", "_id", "internal")
| where SuspiciousFields == true
| summarize 
    AttemptCount = count(),
    UniqueIPs = dcount(ClientIp),
    SamplePayloads = make_set(RequestBody, 5)
  by UserAgent, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| where AttemptCount > 10
| project TimeGenerated, UserAgent, AttemptCount, UniqueIPs, SamplePayloads
| order by AttemptCount desc

index=api_logs sourcetype=access_combined
| where status IN (400, 422)
| spath input=request_body
| eval suspicious_fields=if(match(request_body, "(?i)(isAdmin|role|admin|privileges|_id|internal|system)"), 1, 0)
| where suspicious_fields=1
| stats count as attempts, dc(client_ip) as unique_ips, values(request_body) as sample_payloads by user_agent, _time span=5m
| where attempts > 10
| table _time, user_agent, attempts, unique_ips, sample_payloads
| sort -attempts

http.response.status_code: (400 OR 422) AND 
(
  http.request.body.content: (*isAdmin* OR *role* OR *admin* OR *privileges* OR *_id* OR *internal*)
) AND 
@timestamp >= "now-1h"
| timechart span=5m count() by user_agent.original
| where count > 10

fields @timestamp, @message, statusCode, requestBody, userAgent, sourceIP
| filter statusCode in [400, 422]
| filter requestBody like /isAdmin|role|admin|privileges|_id|internal/i
| stats count() as attempts, count_distinct(sourceIP) as uniqueIPs by userAgent, bin(5m)
| filter attempts > 10
| sort attempts desc
